summary: Frontend-only validation completed on public preview for auth-page + new below-auth inspiration wall. Centered auth form remains focal, curated wall renders cleanly, and signup/login flows still succeed.
action_items: No blocking regressions found in requested auth + showcase scope.
Optional: keep card-level data-testid naming distinct from child label/title/description ids to avoid broad-prefix selector ambiguity in future automated tests.
critical_code_review_comments: AuthShowcaseWall uses overlapping data-testid prefixes (auth-showcase-card-*) for card container and child elements, which can inflate selector matches in automation; not user-facing but increases regression-test fragility.
Auth page composition preserves hierarchy (form section before showcase wall) and does not introduce interaction blocking in tested flow.
seed_data_creation: Created UI signup user for validation: qa_wall_1772717446@example.com
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tested URL: https://quick-site-builder.stage-preview.emergentagent.com. Verified auth page loads at /auth, centered form remains primary visual focus, showcase wall appears below form with 8 loaded images and no broken image elements, signup then logout then login works, and no .error-class messages were detected.
rca of the issue: No defects reproduced in this requested scope.
